description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone material that makes up the rest of the block. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible openings or additional features. The block appears robust and heavy, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its design suggests durability and resistance to weathering, suitable for outdoor use. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over decorative elements.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is solid, speckled gray, and designed for use as a durable urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped grooves running horizontally across the top surface, with the block's solid face visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the grooves again running horizontally and the solid base visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with vertical grooves and a clear view of the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
